vocabulary.definition: To change the form or structure of.

vocabulary.definition: (of a person or their character) Having or showing ambition; wanting a lot of power, honor, respect, superiority, or other distinction.

vocabulary.meaning: 生存：屬於一個人，繼續活著；繼續活著。
vocabulary.definition: Of a person, to continue to live; to remain alive.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "survive" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: To halt something temporarily.
vocabulary.example: The meeting was suspended for lunch.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 午餐會暫停。

vocabulary.definition: A mechanism which can be used to hold a note, as the right pedal on a piano.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "sustain" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 符號：代表一個想法、概念或對象的字元或字形。
vocabulary.definition: A character or glyph representing an idea, concept or object.
vocabulary.example: $ is the symbol for dollars in the US and some other countries.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of pity or sorrow for the suffering or distress of another; compassion.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "sympathy" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: A physical object, something that can be touched.
vocabulary.example: Yes, but what are the tangibles?

vocabulary.meaning: 臨時：一名限時服務；短期員工。
vocabulary.definition: One serving for a limited time; short-term employee.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "temporary" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: A likelihood of behaving in a particular way or going in a particular direction; a tending toward.
vocabulary.example: Denim has a tendency to fade.

vocabulary.definition: The condition of being held in a state between two or more forces, which are acting in opposition to each other.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "tension" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 結束，特別是在不完整的狀態下。
vocabulary.definition: To end, especially in an incomplete state.
vocabulary.example: to terminate a surface by a line

vocabulary.definition: A subject of a talk or an artistic piece; a topic.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "theme" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: By it; by that; by that means, or as a consequence of that.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "thereby" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 徹底：兩個脊之間的溝渠，以排出地表水。
vocabulary.definition: A furrow between two ridges, to drain off the surface water.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "thorough" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: To grow or increase stature; to grow vigorously or luxuriantly, to flourish.
vocabulary.example: Not all animals thrive well in captivity.

vocabulary.definition: To accept hardship without objection.
vocabulary.example: I can tolerate working on Saturdays, but not on Sundays.

vocabulary.meaning: 傳統：文化的一部分，在人與人之間或代與代之間傳遞，可能在細節上因家庭而異，例如慶祝節日的方式。
vocabulary.definition: A part of culture that is passed from person to person or generation to generation, possibly differing in detail from family to family, such as the way to celebrate holidays.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "tradition" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: The act of conveying or removing something from one place, person or thing to another.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "transfer" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: An operation (often an integration) that converts one function into another.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "transform" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 過渡：從一種形式、狀態、風格或地點改變為另一種形式、狀態、風格或地點的過程
vocabulary.definition: The process of change from one form, state, style or place to another.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "transition" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: To send or convey something from one person, place or thing to another.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "transmit" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: (of a material or object) See-through, clear; having the property that light passes through it almost undisturbed, such that one can see through it clearly.
vocabulary.example: The waters of the lake were transparent until the factory dumped waste there.

vocabulary.definition: Awe-inspiring; terrific.
vocabulary.example: Van Beethoven's ninth symphony is a tremendous piece of music.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 範貝多芬的第九交響曲是一首了不起的音樂。

vocabulary.definition: A finger-operated lever used to fire a gun.
vocabulary.example: Just pull the trigger.

vocabulary.definition: The most basic or fundamental of a set of things
vocabulary.example: The concept of "ultimate" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 進行：進入或移動到下方或下方。
vocabulary.definition: To go or move under or beneath.
vocabulary.example: The project is undergoing great changes.

vocabulary.definition: To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.
vocabulary.example: The war efforts were undermined by the constant bickering between the allies.

vocabulary.definition: To take upon oneself; to start, to embark on (a specific task etc.).
vocabulary.example: He undertook to exercise more in future.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 他承諾將來會多運動。

vocabulary.meaning: 制服：一種獨特的服裝，用於識別團體成員。
vocabulary.definition: A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "uniform" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "unique" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: To make use of; to use.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "utilize" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 有充分的根據或合理的，相關的。
vocabulary.definition: Well grounded or justifiable, pertinent.
vocabulary.example: I will believe him as soon as he offers a valid answer.

vocabulary.definition: Something that is variable.
vocabulary.example: There are several variables to consider here.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 這裡有幾個變數需要考慮。

vocabulary.definition: A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "venture" is important in this context.

vocabulary.meaning: 驗證：證明或證明某事的真實性
vocabulary.definition: To substantiate or prove the truth of something
vocabulary.example: The concept of "verify" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: A specific form or variation of something.
vocabulary.example: It's only in the King James Version of the Bible.

vocabulary.definition: A virtual member function of a class.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "virtual" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: Able to be seen.
vocabulary.example: When the sun rises, the world becomes visible.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 當太陽升起時，世界變得清晰可見。

vocabulary.definition: The sense or ability of sight.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "vision" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: Relating to, or characteristic of life.
vocabulary.example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

vocabulary.meaning: 揮發性：容易變成氣體的化學物質或化合物。
vocabulary.definition: A chemical or compound that changes into a gas easily.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "volatile" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: A short piece of music, often having improvisation, played on a solo instrument.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "voluntary" is important in this context.

vocabulary.definition: More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
vocabulary.example: It's okay to get vulnerable every now and again.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 偶爾變得脆弱是可以的。

vocabulary.meaning: 撤回（ withdraw ） ：向後拉、向一旁拉或向外拉。
vocabulary.definition: To pull (something) back, aside, or away.
vocabulary.example: The concept of "withdraw" is important in this context.

slangs.meaning: 祝好運（演出前的祝福語）
slangs.example: You have a big presentation today? Break a leg!
slangs.exampleZh: 你今天有重要的簡報？祝你好運！

slangs.example: I'm exhausted. I think I'll hit the sack early tonight.
slangs.exampleZh: 我累壞了，今晚打算早點睡。

slangs.example: Don't worry about the exam — it'll be a piece of cake.
slangs.exampleZh: 別擔心那場考試——小事一樁。

slangs.example: Come on, spill the beans! What happened at the meeting?
slangs.exampleZh: 快說啊，到底發生了什麼事？

slangs.phrase: cost an arm and a leg
slangs.example: That designer bag costs an arm and a leg.
slangs.exampleZh: 那個名牌包貴得要命。

slangs.example: I hate going to the dentist, but I'll just bite the bullet.
slangs.exampleZh: 我討厭看牙醫，但只好硬著頭皮去了。

slangs.example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.
slangs.exampleZh: 我今天覺得有點不舒服。

slangs.phrase: the ball is in your court
slangs.example: I've made my offer. The ball is in your court now.
slangs.exampleZh: 我已經開出條件了，現在輪到你決定。

slangs.phrase: let the cat out of the bag
slangs.example: She let the cat out of the bag about the surprise party.
slangs.exampleZh: 她不小心把驚喜派對的事說溜嘴了。

slangs.example: I've been burning the midnight oil to finish the project.
slangs.exampleZh: 我為了完成專案一直熬夜。

slangs.example: He only visits once in a blue moon.
slangs.exampleZh: 他很少來，偶爾才來一次。

slangs.example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.
slangs.exampleZh: 丟了那份工作反而因禍得福。

slangs.example: The situation got out of hand quickly.
slangs.exampleZh: 情況很快就失控了。

slangs.example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what happened.
slangs.exampleZh: 別拐彎抹角了，直接告訴我發生什麼事。

slangs.example: She always goes the extra mile for her clients.
slangs.exampleZh: 她總是為客戶格外用心。

slangs.phrase: hit the nail on the head
slangs.meaning: 說得完全正確、一針見血
slangs.example: You hit the nail on the head with that analysis.
slangs.exampleZh: 你的分析一針見血。

slangs.example: We've been working for hours. Let's call it a day.
slangs.exampleZh: 我們已經工作好幾個小時了，收工吧。

slangs.example: Are you serious or just pulling my leg?
slangs.exampleZh: 你是認真的還是在開我玩笑？

slangs.example: Let's make sure we're all on the same page before we start.
slangs.exampleZh: 開始之前先確認大家想法一致。

slangs.meaning: 忍無可忍的最後一根稻草
slangs.example: Being late again was the last straw for his boss.
slangs.exampleZh: 他又遲到了，這是老闆忍耐的最後底線。

slangs.example: Does the name Sarah ring a bell?
slangs.exampleZh: Sarah 這個名字你有印象嗎？

slangs.example: Let me cut to the chase — we need more funding.
slangs.exampleZh: 讓我直說重點——我們需要更多資金。

slangs.example: After three failed attempts, he threw in the towel.
slangs.exampleZh: 失敗三次之後，他認輸了。

slangs.example: I can't wrap my head around quantum physics.
slangs.exampleZh: 我搞不懂量子物理。

slangs.example: Training is tough, but no pain, no gain.
slangs.exampleZh: 訓練很辛苦，但不勞則無獲。

slangs.example: The deal fell through, so we're back to square one.
slangs.exampleZh: 交易破局了，我們又回到原點。

slangs.example: To add insult to injury, it started raining after my car broke down.
slangs.exampleZh: 雪上加霜的是，車子拋錨後又開始下雨。

slangs.example: Keep your chin up — things will get better.
slangs.exampleZh: 振作起來，事情會好轉的。

slangs.example: He got cold feet right before the wedding.
slangs.exampleZh: 他在婚禮前臨陣退縮了。

slangs.example: In a nutshell, we need to cut costs and increase sales.
slangs.exampleZh: 簡而言之，我們需要降低成本、增加銷售。

quotes.text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
quotes.zh: 成就偉大工作的唯一方法，就是熱愛你所做的事。
quotes.author: Steve Jobs

quotes.text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
quotes.zh: 在困難之中，蘊藏著機會。
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
quotes.zh: 不怕慢，只怕站。走得再慢都沒關係，只要不停下來。
quotes.author: Confucius

quotes.text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
quotes.zh: 種一棵樹最好的時機是 20 年前，其次是現在。
quotes.author: Chinese Proverb

quotes.text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
quotes.zh: 你不出手，就百分之百不會進球。
quotes.author: Wayne Gretzky

quotes.text: Strive not to be a success, but rather to be of value.
quotes.zh: 不要只追求成功，要追求成為有價值的人。
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Everything you've ever wanted is on the other side of fear.
quotes.zh: 你想要的一切，都在恐懼的另一邊。
quotes.author: George Addair

quotes.text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
quotes.zh: 成功不是終點，失敗也不是末日，真正重要的是繼續前行的勇氣。
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
quotes.zh: 未來屬於那些相信夢想之美的人。
quotes.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

quotes.text: It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light.
quotes.zh: 越是黑暗的時刻，越要專注去看見光亮。
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.
quotes.zh: 在你所在的地方，用你所擁有的，做你所能做的。
quotes.author: Theodore Roosevelt

quotes.text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
quotes.zh: 相信你可以，你就已經成功了一半。
quotes.author: Theodore Roosevelt

quotes.text: Life is what happens when you're busy making other plans.
quotes.zh: 生活就是當你忙著做其他計劃時發生的事。
quotes.author: John Lennon

quotes.text: The only impossible journey is the one you never begin.
quotes.zh: 唯一不可能的旅程，是你從未開始的那一段。
quotes.author: Tony Robbins

quotes.text: In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on.
quotes.zh: 用三個字就能總結我對人生的體悟：它會繼續。
quotes.author: Robert Frost

quotes.text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
quotes.zh: 我們身後和眼前的，比不上我們內心的力量。
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: The mind is everything. What you think, you become.
quotes.zh: 心念決定一切。你的想法，決定了你會成為什麼樣的人。
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
quotes.zh: 投資知識，獲得的利息最高。
quotes.author: Benjamin Franklin

quotes.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
quotes.zh: 領先的秘訣就是開始行動。
quotes.author: Mark Twain

quotes.text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
quotes.zh: 事情做成之前，看起來似乎都是不可能的。
quotes.author: Nelson Mandela

quotes.text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
quotes.zh: 別盯著時鐘看，要像它一樣持續前進。
quotes.author: Sam Levenson

quotes.text: You are never too old to set another goal or to dream a new dream.
quotes.zh: 你永遠不會太老而無法設定新目標或做新的夢。
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: The best revenge is massive success.
quotes.zh: 最好的報復就是巨大的成功。
quotes.author: Frank Sinatra

quotes.text: What we achieve inwardly will change outer reality.
quotes.zh: 我們內在的改變，終將改變外在的現實。
quotes.author: Plutarch

quotes.text: Happiness is not something ready-made. It comes from your own actions.
quotes.zh: 幸福不是現成的，它來自於你的行動。
quotes.author: Dalai Lama

quotes.text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
quotes.zh: 把你的傷痛轉化為智慧。
quotes.author: Oprah Winfrey

quotes.text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.
quotes.zh: 我們對明天的實現，唯一的限制是今天的懷疑。
quotes.author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

quotes.text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
quotes.zh: 行動時相信你做的事會帶來改變——確實如此。
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
quotes.zh: 教育是你改變世界最強大的武器。
quotes.author: Nelson Mandela

quotes.text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.
quotes.zh: 千里之行，始於足下。
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: So in college, I was a government major, which means I had to write a lot of papers. Now, when a normal student writes a paper, they might spread the work out a little like this. So, you know -- (Laughter)
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 大學的時候， 我主修政治學， 這表示我必須要寫很多論文。 一般學生在寫論文時， 大概會做像這樣的進度表。 你們也知道嘛... （笑聲）

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: you get started maybe a little slowly, but you get enough done in the first week that, with some heavier days later on, everything gets done, things stay civil. (Laughter) And I would want to do that like that.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 一開始慢慢來， 但第一週能完成夠多進度， 後來工作量重了一點， 但事情總會順利完成。 （笑聲） 我也想要這樣子做。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: That would be the plan. I would have it all ready to go, but then, actually, the paper would come along, and then I would kind of do this. (Laughter) And that would happen every single paper. But then came my 90-page senior thesis,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 計畫本來就應該像這樣。 我會把一切通通計畫好， 結果呢，等到真的該寫的時候， 我的計畫會變成這樣。 （笑聲） 每一次寫論文都是這樣。 但終於輪到了我 90 頁的畢業論文，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: a paper you're supposed to spend a year on. And I knew for a paper like that, my normal work flow was not an option. It was way too big a project. So I planned things out, and I decided I kind of had to go something like this.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 這論文照理應該花一年的時間寫。 我知道面對這種論文， 不能用平常的工作方式來做。 因為這實在是太大的工程了。 所以我好好地計畫了一下， 決定要這樣安排。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: This is how the year would go. So I'd start off light, and I'd bump it up in the middle months, and then at the end, I would kick it up into high gear just like a little staircase. How hard could it be to walk up the stairs?
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 這一年大概要長這樣。 所以剛開始做一點就好， 中間幾個月增加一點進度， 最後，我會火力全開， 就跟樓梯一樣嘛。 走樓梯是有多難勒？

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: No big deal, right? But then, the funniest thing happened. Those first few months? They came and went, and I couldn't quite do stuff. So we had an awesome new revised plan. (Laughter) And then -- (Laughter)
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 輕而易舉，對吧？ 可是，好笑的事情發生了。 前面幾個月， 突然就飛走了， 我根本做不了什麼。 所以我就修改成超棒的新進度表。 （笑聲） 然後... （笑聲）

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: But then those middle months actually went by, and I didn't really write words, and so we were here. And then two months turned into one month, which turned into two weeks. And one day I woke up with three days until the deadline,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 然後中間的月份也飛走了， 我真的也沒擠出幾個字， 所以就變成這樣。 然後兩個月變成一個月， 之後剩兩週。 有天我醒來， 發現只剩三天就要交論文了，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: still not having written a word, and so I did the only thing I could: I wrote 90 pages over 72 hours, pulling not one but two all-nighters -- humans are not supposed to pull two all-nighters -- sprinted across campus,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 我還是連一個字都沒生出來， 所以我能做的只有一件事： 我用 72 小時生出 90 頁的論文， 不只熬夜了一天，是連續兩天── 人真的不能連熬兩天夜── 完成後我衝到校園，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: dove in slow motion, and got it in just at the deadline. I thought that was the end of everything. But a week later I get a call, and it's the school. And they say, "Is this Tim Urban?" And I say, "Yeah."
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 慢動作往前撲， 正好在最後一刻趕上。 我以為這樣就結束了。 但一週後，我接到電話， 是學校打來的。 他們說：「請問你是提姆．厄本嗎？」 我說：「對。」

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And they say, "We need to talk about your thesis." And I say, "OK." And they say, "It's the best one we've ever seen." (Laughter) (Applause) That did not happen. (Laughter) It was a very, very bad thesis.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 他們說：「我們得談談你的論文。」 我說：「好。」 他們說： 「我們從沒見過這麼棒的論文。」 （笑聲） （掌聲） 並沒有發生這種事。 （笑聲） 我寫得超級無敵爛。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: (Laughter) I just wanted to enjoy that one moment when all of you thought, "This guy is amazing!" (Laughter) No, no, it was very, very bad. Anyway, today I'm a writer-blogger guy. I write the blog Wait But Why.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: （笑聲） 我只是想要享受一下你們心想： 「這傢伙太厲害了吧！」那瞬間。 （笑聲） 並沒有發生這種事，我寫得很糟。 總之，我現在是個部落客， 部落格名稱是「等一下但是為什麼」。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And a couple of years ago, I decided to write about procrastination. My behavior has always perplexed the non-procrastinators around me, and I wanted to explain to the non-procrastinators of the world what goes on in the heads of procrastinators,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 幾年前，我決定開始寫拖拖拉拉的事。 我身邊不會拖拖拉拉的朋友 總對我的行為感到非常不解， 所以我想解釋給全世界 所有不會拖拖拉拉的人 看我們愛拖的人腦袋裡在想什麼，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: and why we are the way we are. Now, I had a hypothesis that the brains of procrastinators were actually different than the brains of other people. And to test this, I found an MRI lab that actually let me scan both my brain
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 以及我們為何如此。 現在呢，我有個假設， 那就是拖延者的大腦 其實跟其它人不一樣。 為了證實這點， 我找到一個核磁共振實驗室， 同意讓我掃描自己的大腦，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: and the brain of a proven non-procrastinator, so I could compare them. I actually brought them here to show you today. I want you to take a look carefully to see if you can notice a difference. I know that if you're not a trained brain expert,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 以及一位不會拖延者的大腦， 然後比較兩者的差異。 我今天特地把掃描結果帶來給你們看。 請仔細看看有沒有不一樣。 如果你們不是專家的話， 可能沒有那麼容易看出來，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: it's not that obvious, but just take a look, OK? So here's the brain of a non-procrastinator. (Laughter) Now ... here's my brain. (Laughter) There is a difference. Both brains have a Rational Decision-Maker in them,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 不過就稍微看一下好嗎？ 這是不會拖延者的大腦。 （笑聲） 然後呢... 這是我的大腦。 （笑聲） 果然不一樣吧。 兩個大腦裡都有理性決策者，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: but the procrastinator's brain also has an Instant Gratification Monkey. Now, what does this mean for the procrastinator? Well, it means everything's fine until this happens. [This is a perfect time to get some work done.] [Nope!]
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 但拖延者的大腦 同時還有一隻及時行樂猴。 這對拖延者來說是什麼意思？ 在這種狀況發生前，一切都沒事。 [現在是該做事的最好時機喔。] [才不是！]

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: So the Rational Decision-Maker will make the rational decision to do something productive, but the Monkey doesn't like that plan, so he actually takes the wheel, and he says, "Actually, let's read the entire Wikipedia page
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 所以理性決策者會做出理性決定 該做點事情了， 但猴子並不喜歡這決定， 所以就搶過方向盤， 然後說：「不然我們看完維基百科上

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: of the Nancy Kerrigan/ Tonya Harding scandal, because I just remembered that that happened. (Laughter) Then -- (Laughter) Then we're going to go over to the fridge, to see if there's anything new in there since 10 minutes ago.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 花式滑冰選手南西克里根 和坦雅哈丁的整篇醜聞報導吧， 因為我剛剛想到有這件事。」 （笑聲） 然後... （笑聲） 然後我們再去看一下冰箱， 看看跟十分鐘前有什麼不一樣。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: After that, we're going to go on a YouTube spiral that starts with videos of Richard Feynman talking about magnets and ends much, much later with us watching interviews with Justin Bieber's mom. (Laughter)
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 再來去看看 YouTube 上的一連串影片， 就從理察．費曼談論磁鐵開始， 一路看看看看看， 最後看到小賈斯丁他媽的訪問影片。 （笑聲）

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: "All of that's going to take a while, so we're not going to really have room on the schedule for any work today. Sorry!" (Sigh) Now, what is going on here? The Instant Gratification Monkey does not seem like a guy
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 「這一切都需要花點時間， 所以今天沒有辦法做正事了。 拍謝啦！」 （嘆） 所以到底發生什麼事？ 你應該不會希望及時行樂猴

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: you want behind the wheel. He lives entirely in the present moment. He has no memory of the past, no knowledge of the future, and he only cares about two things: easy and fun. Now, in the animal world, that works fine.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 控制方向盤。 他只活在當下。 他沒有過去的記憶， 不曉得未來會怎樣， 他只在乎兩件事： 輕鬆、有趣。 在動物的世界裡，這樣做完全行得通。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: If you're a dog and you spend your whole life doing nothing other than easy and fun things, you're a huge success! (Laughter) And to the Monkey, humans are just another animal species. You have to keep well-slept, well-fed and propagating into the next generation,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 如果你是隻狗， 一輩子只做輕鬆有趣的事，不做別的， 那你就成功了啊！ （笑聲） 對猴子來說， 人類只是另一種動物物種。 只要睡飽、吃好，繁殖下一代就行了，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: which in tribal times might have worked OK. But, if you haven't noticed, now we're not in tribal times. We're in an advanced civilization, and the Monkey does not know what that is. Which is why we have another guy in our brain,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 這在以前部落時代還可以。 不過，如果你沒注意到的話， 我們時代已經不一樣了。 我們處在先進文明的世界， 但猴子不知道這是什麼情況， 所以我們腦中才有另一個

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: the Rational Decision-Maker, who gives us the ability to do things no other animal can do. We can visualize the future. We can see the big picture. We can make long-term plans. And he wants to take all of that into account.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 理性決策者存在， 他讓我們有能力 做其它動物做不到的事。 我們可以想像未來； 可以綜觀大局； 可以做長遠規劃。 他想要把這些都納入考慮，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And he wants to just have us do whatever makes sense to be doing right now. Now, sometimes it makes sense to be doing things that are easy and fun, like when you're having dinner or going to bed or enjoying well-earned leisure time.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 所以他只要我們做 當下合理的事情就好。 有時候，做輕鬆有趣的事情很合理， 像是吃晚餐或是上床睡覺， 或是享受應得的休閒時光。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: That's why there's an overlap. Sometimes they agree. But other times, it makes much more sense to be doing things that are harder and less pleasant, for the sake of the big picture. And that's when we have a conflict.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 這就是為什麼有中間重疊的部分。 有時候他們意見會達成一致。 但其它時候，做比較難的事情 或是比較不好玩的事合理許多， 這是為了大局著想。 這就是發生衝突的時候了。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And for the procrastinator, that conflict tends to end a certain way every time, leaving him spending a lot of time in this orange zone, an easy and fun place that's entirely out of the Makes Sense circle.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 對拖延者來說， 衝突幾乎每次都會以同樣的方式作結， 讓他有很多時間待在橘色這區， 既輕鬆又好玩的地方， 可是完全在合理區之外，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: I call it the Dark Playground. (Laughter) Now, the Dark Playground is a place that all of you procrastinators out there know very well. It's where leisure activities happen at times when leisure activities are not supposed to be happening.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 我稱之為暗黑遊樂場。 （笑聲） 暗黑遊樂場這個地方 是所有拖延者都心有戚戚焉的。 這是不該悠哉時， 卻做悠哉的事情時，會出現的地方。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: The fun you have in the Dark Playground isn't actually fun, because it's completely unearned, and the air is filled with guilt, dread, anxiety, self-hatred -- all of those good procrastinator feelings.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 暗黑遊樂場的玩樂並非真的玩樂， 因為完全不是努力賺來的， 而且空氣中瀰漫著內疚、 擔心、焦慮、自我厭惡── 所有這些拖延的良好感受。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And the question is, in this situation, with the Monkey behind the wheel, how does the procrastinator ever get himself over here to this blue zone, a less pleasant place, but where really important things happen?
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 問題是，在這種情況下， 由猴子掌舵時， 拖延者要怎麼過到藍色這個 比較不好玩，但重要事情發生的地方？

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: Well, turns out the procrastinator has a guardian angel, someone who's always looking down on him and watching over him in his darkest moments -- someone called the Panic Monster. (Laughter) Now, the Panic Monster is dormant most of the time,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 嗯，原來拖延者有個守護天使， 在最黑暗的時候， 永遠照看他、守護他的人── 這個人就叫做恐慌怪獸。 （笑聲） 恐慌怪獸大多時候都在休眠，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: but he suddenly wakes up anytime a deadline gets too close or there's danger of public embarrassment, a career disaster or some other scary consequence. And importantly, he's the only thing the Monkey is terrified of.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 但等到交期接近時會突然醒來， 或是快要遇到公開出糗、 事業危機或其它恐怖結果時驚醒。 更重要的是，他是猴子 唯一會懼怕的東西。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: Now, he became very relevant in my life pretty recently, because the people of TED reached out to me about six months ago and invited me to do a TED Talk. (Laughter) Now, of course, I said yes. It's always been a dream of mine to have done a TED Talk in the past.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 他最近跟我的生活息息相關， 因為 TED 的人大概半年前聯絡我， 邀請我來 TED 演講。 （笑聲） 當然，我說好。 「以前」做 TED 演講 一直都是我的夢想。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: (Laughter) (Applause) But in the middle of all this excitement, the Rational Decision-Maker seemed to have something else on his mind. He was saying, "Are we clear on what we just accepted? Do we get what's going to be now happening one day in the future?
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: （笑聲） （掌聲） 興奮之餘， 理性決策者似乎心事重重。 他說：「我們知道剛剛答應了什麼嗎？ 我們知道未來某一天會發生什麼事嗎？

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: We need to sit down and work on this right now." And the Monkey said, "Totally agree, but let's just open Google Earth and zoom in to the bottom of India, like 200 feet above the ground, and scroll up for two and a half hours til we get to the top of the country,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 我們需要坐下來， 現在就著手進行這件事。」 然後猴子說：「完全同意， 不過我們先打開 Google Earth， 縮放到印度底部大概地平面上兩百呎， 然後花兩個半小時， 一直看到印度頂部， 這樣才能好好了解一下整個印度。」

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: so we can get a better feel for India." (Laughter) So that's what we did that day. (Laughter) As six months turned into four and then two and then one, the people of TED decided to release the speakers.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 這樣才能好好了解一下整個印度。」 （笑聲） 所以我們那天就做了這件事。 （笑聲） 六個月變成四個月， 又變成兩個月，然後一個月， TED 的人決定公佈講者名單。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And I opened up the website, and there was my face staring right back at me. And guess who woke up? (Laughter) So the Panic Monster starts losing his mind, and a few seconds later, the whole system's in mayhem.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 我打開網站後，看到我自己的臉， 就在我正前方。 這時候，猜猜看誰醒了？ （笑聲） 恐慌怪獸開始發瘋， 幾秒鐘之後，整個系統一片混亂。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: (Laughter) And the Monkey -- remember, he's terrified of the Panic Monster -- boom, he's up the tree! And finally, finally, the Rational Decision-Maker can take the wheel and I can start working on the talk.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: （笑聲） 然後，還記得猴子怕死恐慌怪獸了嗎？ 砰！他跑到樹上了！ 最後， 最後，理性決策者可以搶過方向盤， 我也可以開始準備這演講。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: Now, the Panic Monster explains all kinds of pretty insane procrastinator behavior, like how someone like me could spend two weeks unable to start the opening sentence of a paper, and then miraculously find the unbelievable work ethic
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 恐慌怪獸解釋了 各種瘋狂的拖延行為， 例如像我這樣的人怎麼會花兩週的時間 就是無法寫出文章開頭的幾句話， 然後神奇地發現自己 驚人的職業道德，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: to stay up all night and write eight pages. And this entire situation, with the three characters -- this is the procrastinator's system. It's not pretty, but in the end, it works. This is what I decided to write about on the blog a couple of years ago.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 並熬夜寫出八頁的文章。 這三種角色存在的整個情況， 就是拖延者的系統。 是不漂亮，但終究是能發揮作用的。 這就是我幾年前決定寫部落格的事情。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: When I did, I was amazed by the response. Literally thousands of emails came in, from all different kinds of people from all over the world, doing all different kinds of things. These are people who were nurses, bankers, painters, engineers
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 寫了之後，讀者的回應讓我很驚訝。 真的有幾千封電子郵件進來， 世界各地的千百種人寫信給我， 他們來自各行各業， 其中有護士、銀行家、畫家、工程師，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: and lots and lots of PhD students. (Laughter) And they were all writing, saying the same thing: "I have this problem too." But what struck me was the contrast between the light tone of the post and the heaviness of these emails.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 還有超多、超多的博士生。 （笑聲） 大家都說一樣的事： 「我也有這個問題。」 但我驚訝的是，我寫得很輕鬆的文章 以及收到的沉重信件形成強烈對比。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: These people were writing with intense frustration about what procrastination had done to their lives, about what this Monkey had done to them. And I thought about this, and I said, well, if the procrastinator's system works, then what's going on?
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 這些人都非常挫折， 覺得拖延對生活有很大影響， 對猴子的行為感到挫敗。 我想了想之後說： 嗯，要是拖延者的系統有用， 那怎麼會發生這種情況？

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: Why are all of these people in such a dark place? Well, it turns out that there's two kinds of procrastination. Everything I've talked about today, the examples I've given, they all have deadlines. And when there's deadlines,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 為什麼這些人還是灰頭土臉？ 原來啊，拖延有兩種。 我今天所講的、剛剛所舉的例子 都是有交期的。 有交期的時候， 拖延的效果就會被控制在短期，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: the effects of procrastination are contained to the short term because the Panic Monster gets involved. But there's a second kind of procrastination that happens in situations when there is no deadline.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 拖延的效果就會被控制在短期， 因為恐慌怪獸會出面。 但還有另一種拖延， 就是沒有交期的情況。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: So if you wanted a career where you're a self-starter -- something in the arts, something entrepreneurial -- there's no deadlines on those things at first, because nothing's happening, not until you've gone out and done the hard work
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 所以如果你想要自己創業── 想往藝術發展、創立公司── 這些事剛開始是沒有交期的， 因為什麼事都沒有發生， 除非是已經開始著手， 努力了一陣子，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: to get momentum, get things going. There's also all kinds of important things outside of your career that don't involve any deadlines, like seeing your family or exercising and taking care of your health,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 有衝勁繼續往下做的時候。 除了事業以外， 也有各種重要的事情 是沒有期限的， 像是跟家人相聚 或是運動、照顧身體、 經營人際或戀愛關係，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: working on your relationship or getting out of a relationship that isn't working. Now if the procrastinator's only mechanism of doing these hard things is the Panic Monster, that's a problem, because in all of these non-deadline situations,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 經營人際或戀愛關係， 或是終結一段沒有進展的關係。 如果拖延者做這些難事的唯一機制 是恐慌怪獸，那問題就來了， 因為以上這些事情都沒有交期，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: the Panic Monster doesn't show up. He has nothing to wake up for, so the effects of procrastination, they're not contained; they just extend outward forever. And it's this long-term kind of procrastination
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 所以恐慌怪獸根本不會出現。 他沒有理由醒來， 所以拖延的影響並沒有被控制住， 而是一直無限延後。 這種長期的拖延

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: that's much less visible and much less talked about than the funnier, short-term deadline-based kind. It's usually suffered quietly and privately. And it can be the source of a huge amount of long-term unhappiness, and regrets.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 較難查覺， 且跟比較好笑的短期拖延相比 也較少人談論， 所以受苦的情況比較低調、私密。 也會延伸出 長期的不快樂以及悔恨。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: And I thought, that's why those people are emailing, and that's why they're in such a bad place. It's not that they're cramming for some project. It's that long-term procrastination has made them feel like a spectator,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 所以我想，這就是這些人 寫信給我的關係， 也是為什麼他們這麼悽慘。 並不是他們一直積累著某些計畫不做， 只是長期拖延讓他們 有時感覺就像旁觀者，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: at times, in their own lives. The frustration is not that they couldn't achieve their dreams; it's that they weren't even able to start chasing them. So I read these emails and I had a little bit of an epiphany --
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 只是長期拖延讓他們 有時感覺就像旁觀者， 從旁觀看著自己的人生。 挫折的並不是他們無法完成夢想， 而是根本無法踏出逐夢的第一步。 所以我看這些信件時， 突然有種領悟──

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: that I don't think non-procrastinators exist. That's right -- I think all of you are procrastinators. Now, you might not all be a mess, like some of us, (Laughter) and some of you may have a healthy relationship with deadlines,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 那就是不拖延的人根本不存在。 沒錯，我覺得你們全都是拖延者。 或許你沒有像我們某些人 症狀那麼嚴重... （笑聲） 或許有些人跟交期相處得比較好，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: but remember: the Monkey's sneakiest trick is when the deadlines aren't there. Now, I want to show you one last thing. I call this a Life Calendar. That's one box for every week of a 90-year life. That's not that many boxes,
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 但是請記得：猴子最陰險的招數 就是沒有交期的時候。 我想要再給你們看最後一個東西。 我稱這個為生命日曆。 每個格子代表一星期，總共 90 歲的生命。 其實真正的格子並沒有這麼多，

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: especially since we've already used a bunch of those. So I think we need to all take a long, hard look at that calendar. We need to think about what we're really procrastinating on, because everyone is procrastinating on something in life.
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 特別是我們已經用掉很多格了。 所以我認為，大家都需要 認真仔細地看著這個日曆。 我們需要思考真正在拖延的是什麼， 因為每個人都拖延著不同的事情。

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: We need to stay aware of the Instant Gratification Monkey. That's a job for all of us. And because there's not that many boxes on there, it's a job that should probably start today. Well, maybe not today, but ...
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: 我們需要意識到及時行樂猴的存在。 這是所有人的任務。 因為現在人生方格已經不多了， 所以這個任務應該今天就開始。 呃，或許不用今天就開始啦，不過...

tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.en: (Laughter) You know. Sometime soon. Thank you. (Applause)
tim_urban_inside_the_mind_of_a_master_pr.zh: （笑聲） 你懂的， 很快啦。 謝謝。 （掌聲）

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: So, I'll start with this: a couple years ago, an event planner called me because I was going to do a speaking event. And she called, and she said, "I'm really struggling with how to write about you on the little flyer."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 先講個故事 幾年前，有個活動策劃打電話給我 因為我要辦一個講座 她打給我說 「我實在想不出來」 「在傳單上要怎麼介紹你」 我想說「為什麼想不出來？」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And I thought, "Well, what's the struggle?" And she said, "Well, I saw you speak, and I'm going to call you a researcher, I think, but I'm afraid if I call you a researcher, no one will come, because they'll think you're boring and irrelevant."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我想說「為什麼想不出來？」 她答：「看過你演講」 「我想應該稱呼你為研究員」 「但我怕這樣寫，沒有人會來聽演講」 「因為研究員都很無聊、無關緊要」 (笑聲) 好吧

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: (Laughter) And I was like, "Okay." And she said, "But the thing I liked about your talk is you're a storyteller. So I think what I'll do is just call you a storyteller." And of course, the academic, insecure part of me
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 好吧 然後她說「但我喜歡你演講的原因」 「是因為很像在說故事」 「所以我要稱你為說書人」 我內心學術、沒安全感那面就跳出來 「妳要叫我什麼？」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: was like, "You're going to call me a what?" And she said, "I'm going to call you a storyteller." And I was like, "Why not 'magic pixie'?" (Laughter) I was like, "Let me think about this for a second." I tried to call deep on my courage.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 「妳要叫我什麼？」 她說：「說故事的人阿」 我心想：「何不乾脆叫我魔法小精靈？」 (笑聲) 「讓我想一下」 我鼓起勇氣 我想：我是說書人

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And I thought, you know, I am a storyteller. I'm a qualitative researcher. I collect stories; that's what I do. And maybe stories are just data with a soul. And maybe I'm just a storyteller. And so I said, "You know what?
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我想：我是說書人 我是定性研究員 我工作就是搜集故事 也許故事就是有靈魂的數據 或許我真的就是說書人 我就說：「好吧」 「你就叫我研究說書員」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: Why don't you just say I'm a researcher-storyteller." And she went, "Ha ha. There's no such thing." (Laughter) So I'm a researcher-storyteller, and I'm going to talk to you today -- we're talking about expanding perception --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 「你就叫我研究說書員」 她就回：「哈，沒這東西」 (笑聲) 所以我是個研究說書員 我今天要告訴各位的 是關於看法的擴展 我要分享給各位幾個故事

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: and so I want to talk to you and tell some stories about a piece of my research that fundamentally expanded my perception and really actually changed the way that I live and love and work and parent. And this is where my story starts.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我要分享給各位幾個故事 是我研究的一部份 這部份徹底的擴展了我的觀點 並確實改變了我生活、愛、 工作、為人父母的過程 這是我故事的開始

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: When I was a young researcher, doctoral student, my first year, I had a research professor who said to us, "Here's the thing, if you cannot measure it, it does not exist." And I thought he was just sweet-talking me.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 當時我還是個年輕的研究員，念博士 第一年，我有個教授 告訴我們 「是這樣的」 「你無法測量的，就不存在」 我想他只是講些好聽話而已 我就說：「真的嗎？」他回：「千真萬確」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: I was like, "Really?" and he was like, "Absolutely." And so you have to understand that I have a bachelor's and a master's in social work, and I was getting my Ph.D. in social work, so my entire academic career
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我就說：「真的嗎？」他回：「千真萬確」 你們要明白 我學士、碩士都念社工 我的博士學位也是社工 所以我的學術事業 都圍繞著

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: was surrounded by people who kind of believed in the "life's messy, love it." And I'm more of the, "life's messy, clean it up, organize it and put it into a bento box." (Laughter) And so to think that I had found my way, to found a career that takes me --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 都圍繞著 一些相信 「生活很混亂，愛它吧」的人 但我是那種「生活很混亂」 「那就收拾整齊」 「並收進便當盒裡」的人 (笑聲) 我想我找到我的路 發現一個能帶著我的事業 社工常說的一句話

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: really, one of the big sayings in social work is, "Lean into the discomfort of the work." And I'm like, knock discomfort upside the head and move it over and get all A's. That was my mantra. So I was very excited about this.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 社工常說的一句話 偎緊工作令人不舒服的部份 我則是那種，遇到不愉快就趕緊 想辦法對付他並拿A 我這樣催眠自己 對此很期待 我想，這就是我要做的大事了

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And so I thought, you know what, this is the career for me, because I am interested in some messy topics. But I want to be able to make them not messy. I want to understand them. I want to hack into these things that I know are important
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我想，這就是我要做的大事了 因為我對這些混亂的話題有興趣 但我想讓他們變得不混亂 我想搞懂他們 我想徹底研究這些 我知道很重要的事 並把秘密找出來分享給大家

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: and lay the code out for everyone to see. So where I started was with connection. Because, by the time you're a social worker for 10 years, what you realize is that connection is why we're here. It's what gives purpose and meaning to our lives.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 並把秘密找出來分享給大家 所以我從"連結"開始 因為，當了十年社工 你會理解到 連結就是我們都在此的原因 這是我們生命被賦予意義的東西

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: This is what it's all about. It doesn't matter whether you talk to people who work in social justice, mental health and abuse and neglect, what we know is that connection, the ability to feel connected, is --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 一切都與這相關 不管你跟哪個領域的人聊天 不論是社會公平、心理健康、 受虐與疏忽照顧 我們知道，連結 感受到彼此的連結是 在神經生物學來說，是我們天生的

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: neurobiologically that's how we're wired -- it's why we're here. So I thought, you know what, I'm going to start with connection. Well, you know that situation where you get an evaluation from your boss,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 在神經生物學來說，是我們天生的 我們在此的原因 所以我想，我要以連結開始研究 我想你們都經歷過 老闆給的工作評價 可能會是37件極佳的表現

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: and she tells you 37 things that you do really awesome, and one "opportunity for growth?" (Laughter) And all you can think about is that opportunity for growth, right? Well, apparently this is the way my work went as well,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 可能會是37件極佳的表現 但有一件是 -- 尚待改進 (笑聲) 你腦子只會想這那尚待改進的事 我的研究也是這樣 因為當你問人們關於愛的故事

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: because, when you ask people about love, they tell you about heartbreak. When you ask people about belonging, they'll tell you their most excruciating experiences of being excluded. And when you ask people about connection,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 因為當你問人們關於愛的故事 他們會告訴你心碎的故事 當你問人們關於歸屬的故事 他們會告訴你那些他們被排除在外 的椎心經驗 因為當你問人們關於連結的故事 他們會告訴你那些分離的故事

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: the stories they told me were about disconnection. So very quickly -- really about six weeks into this research -- I ran into this unnamed thing that absolutely unraveled connection in a way that I didn't understand or had never seen.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 他們會告訴你那些分離的故事 所以很快的，研究開始的六週後 就是這件不知如何命名的事 讓連結瓦解 我不懂也從未見過 所以我將研究暫停

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And so I pulled back out of the research and thought, I need to figure out what this is. And it turned out to be shame. And shame is really easily understood as the fear of disconnection: Is there something about me that, if other people know it or see it,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 所以我將研究暫停 我想，我一定要知道這是為什麼 原來就是羞恥 羞恥很好理解 就是害怕失去連結 我的哪些部份 如果外人知道了 會不會不想與我往來

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: that I won't be worthy of connection? The things I can tell you about it: It's universal; we all have it. The only people who don't experience shame have no capacity for human empathy or connection. No one wants to talk about it,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 會不會不想與我往來 羞恥，我可以告訴你們 是普遍存在的，我們都有 那些沒有羞恥心的人 沒有同情心與人類連結 沒人想談羞恥 越不談感受越大

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: and the less you talk about it, the more you have it. What underpinned this shame, this "I'm not good enough," -- which, we all know that feeling: "I'm not blank enough. I'm not thin enough, rich enough, beautiful enough, smart enough, promoted enough."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 越不談感受越大 鞏固羞恥的東西 這種「我不夠好」的感受 我想我們都經歷過 「不夠徹底、不夠纖瘦」 「不夠有錢、不夠漂亮、不夠聰明」 「不夠有才」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: The thing that underpinned this was excruciating vulnerability. This idea of, in order for connection to happen, we have to allow ourselves to be seen, really seen. And you know how I feel about vulnerability. I hate vulnerability.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 「不夠有才」 支撐這種 極痛苦的脆弱感的 是這種 為了要有連結 我們必須讓自己被看見 真的被看見 各位都知道，我恨脆弱 所以我想，這是我

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And so I thought, this is my chance to beat it back with my measuring stick. I'm going in, I'm going to figure this stuff out, I'm going to spend a year, I'm going to totally deconstruct shame, I'm going to understand how vulnerability works,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 所以我想，這是我 用量尺贏回來的機會 我一定要搞懂這東西 我要花一年的時間，破解羞恥 我一定要了解脆弱是如何運作 我要打敗它

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: and I'm going to outsmart it. So I was ready, and I was really excited. As you know, it's not going to turn out well. (Laughter) You know this. So, I could tell you a lot about shame, but I'd have to borrow everyone else's time.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我要打敗它 我準備好了，也很興奮 各位也知道，結果沒有很好 (笑聲) 你們都懂 關於羞恥我可以講很多 但會佔用到其他講者的時間 我可以告訴你的是，研究最終

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: But here's what I can tell you that it boils down to -- and this may be one of the most important things that I've ever learned in the decade of doing this research. My one year turned into six years: Thousands of stories, hundreds of long interviews, focus groups.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我可以告訴你的是，研究最終 這是我十年的研究所學到最重要 的其中一件事 我的一年 變成六年 數千個故事 數百個面談，團體會晤 某階段還有人寄給我日記手札

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: At one point, people were sending me journal pages and sending me their stories -- thousands of pieces of data in six years. And I kind of got a handle on it. I kind of understood, this is what shame is, this is how it works.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 某階段還有人寄給我日記手札 告訴我他們的故事 六年間有數千筆數據 我也大概弄懂了 我有點明白羞恥是什麼 是怎麼運作的

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: I wrote a book, I published a theory, but something was not okay -- and what it was is that, if I roughly took the people I interviewed and divided them into people who really have a sense of worthiness --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我寫了本書 出版了個理論 但有件事還是不對 就是 我將我訪問過的人 分為兩組 一組是認為自己有價值的人 -- 這也是核心的部份

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: that's what this comes down to, a sense of worthiness -- they have a strong sense of love and belonging -- and folks who struggle for it, and folks who are always wondering if they're good enough. There was only one variable that separated
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 這也是核心的部份 個人價值感 -- 這些人有很強的愛與歸屬感 另一組是掙扎的人 他們總想自己是否夠好 兩組間只有一個可變因素 有強烈愛與歸屬感的人

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: the people who have a strong sense of love and belonging and the people who really struggle for it. And that was, the people who have a strong sense of love and belonging believe they're worthy of love and belonging.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 有強烈愛與歸屬感的人 與掙扎的人之間 只有一個可變因素 這就是，那些 有強烈愛與歸屬感的人 相信他們是值得愛與歸屬的

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: That's it. They believe they're worthy. And to me, the hard part of the one thing that keeps us out of connection is our fear that we're not worthy of connection, was something that, personally and professionally,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 就這樣 他們相信這是值得的 對我來說，困難的部份 唯一讓我們無法連結的部份 就是害怕我們不值得彼此連結 這個在個人以及工作上來說 我覺得我應該要更精確理解

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: I felt like I needed to understand better. So what I did is I took all of the interviews where I saw worthiness, where I saw people living that way, and just looked at those. What do these people have in common?
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我覺得我應該要更精確理解 所以我 將所有的訪談裡 看得出個人價值感的案例拿出來 只看這些人 這些人有什麼共同點？ 我對辦公用品有點龜毛

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: I have a slight office supply addiction, but that's another talk. So I had a manila folder, and I had a Sharpie, and I was like, what am I going to call this research? And the first words that came to my mind were "whole-hearted."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我對辦公用品有點龜毛 但這是另一個故事了 我拿了個分類文件夾、一隻簽字筆 我就想，這研究要怎麼稱呼? 我想到的第一個字就是 全心全意

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: These are whole-hearted people, living from this deep sense of worthiness. So I wrote at the top of the manila folder, and I started looking at the data. In fact, I did it first in a four-day, very intensive data analysis,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 這些都是全心付出的人，以強烈價值感活著 所以我在資料夾上這樣寫 並開始看我所找到的資料 事實上 我所做的是 前四天都密集研究資料

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: where I went back, pulled the interviews, the stories, pulled the incidents. What's the theme? What's the pattern? My husband left town with the kids because I always go into this Jackson Pollock crazy thing,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 前四天都密集研究資料 回到最初的訪問、故事、事件 主題是什麼？模式是什麼？ 我老公帶孩子出遠門 因為我整個廢寢忘食 我狂寫資料

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: where I'm just writing and in my researcher mode. And so here's what I found. What they had in common was a sense of courage. And I want to separate courage and bravery for you for a minute. Courage, the original definition of courage,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我狂寫資料 進入我"研究員模式" 我的研究結果是這樣的 他們共有的 是勇氣(courage) 我想簡單解釋勇氣(courage)與勇敢(bravery)的不同 勇氣最初的定義 最早變成英語的時候

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: when it first came into the English language -- it's from the Latin word "cor," meaning "heart" -- and the original definition was to tell the story of who you are with your whole heart. And so these folks had, very simply, the courage to be imperfect.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 最早變成英語的時候 是拉丁字源cor，表示"心(heart)" 而它最初的定義 就是全心全意講述關於你自己的故事 所以這些人 有著承認不完美 的勇氣

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: They had the compassion to be kind to themselves first and then to others, because, as it turns out, we can't practice compassion with other people if we can't treat ourselves kindly. And the last was they had connection, and -- this was the hard part --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 他們有同情心 對自己好之後對別人好 因為，後來發現，你要對別人有同情心 就一定要先對自己好 最後，他們都有著連結 這是困難的部份 就是"真實性"的結果

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: as a result of authenticity, they were willing to let go of who they thought they should be in order to be who they were, which you have to absolutely do that for connection. The other thing that they had in common was this:
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 就是"真實性"的結果 他們願意放下他們想成為的自己 為了做真正的自己 而為了與人連結 就必須這麼做 他們的另一個共通點 是這個

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: They fully embraced vulnerability. They believed that what made them vulnerable made them beautiful. They didn't talk about vulnerability being comfortable, nor did they really talk about it being excruciating --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 他們坦蕩的接受脆弱 他們相信 讓他們脆弱的 會讓他們美麗 他們並不是說脆弱 是很自在的事 也不是很痛心的事 從我早期"羞恥"訪問中發現的

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: as I had heard it earlier in the shame interviewing. They just talked about it being necessary. They talked about the willingness to say, "I love you" first ... the willingness to do something where there are no guarantees ...
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 從我早期"羞恥"訪問中發現的 他們認為脆弱是必須的 他們談到願意先說 「我愛你」 願意 去做那些 不保證美好的事

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: the willingness to breathe through waiting for the doctor to call after your mammogram. They're willing to invest in a relationship that may or may not work out. They thought this was fundamental. I personally thought it was betrayal.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 願意 在乳房X光檢查後深呼吸 等待醫生回電 願意投入一場戀愛 不論結果好不好 他們認為這是很基本的 我個人認為這是背叛

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: I could not believe I had pledged allegiance to research, where our job -- you know, the definition of research is to control and predict, to study phenomena for the explicit reason to control and predict.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 因為我發了誓要 做研究 研究的定義 就是控制和預測、研究現象 就這麼簡單 控制和預測

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And now my mission to control and predict had turned up the answer that the way to live is with vulnerability and to stop controlling and predicting. This led to a little breakdown -- (Laughter) -- which actually looked more like this.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 控制和預測 所以我原本的任務 控制和預測 變成發現到人們都與脆弱一起生活 並停止控制和預測 後來導致我有點小崩潰 (笑聲) 應該是這樣 (笑聲)

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: (Laughter) And it did. I call it a breakdown; my therapist calls it a spiritual awakening. (Laughter) A spiritual awakening sounds better than breakdown, but I assure you, it was a breakdown. And I had to put my data away and go find a therapist.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: (笑聲) 沒錯 我稱為崩潰，我心理醫師稱為心靈覺醒 心靈覺醒好聽多了 不過我確定就是崩潰沒錯 所以我把工作放下，去找心理醫師 告訴你吧，當你打電話給朋友並問他們：

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: Let me tell you something: you know who you are when you call your friends and say, "I think I need to see somebody. Do you have any recommendations?" Because about five of my friends were like, "Wooo, I wouldn't want to be your therapist."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 告訴你吧，當你打電話給朋友並問他們： 「我想我需要看心理醫生了，你有建議誰嗎？」的時候 就瞭解自己是誰了 我五個朋友都說 「真慶幸我不是你心理醫師」 (笑聲)

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: (Laughter) I was like, "What does that mean?" And they're like, "I'm just saying, you know. Don't bring your measuring stick." (Laughter) I was like, "Okay." So I found a therapist. My first meeting with her, Diana --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: (笑聲) 我就說：「你這什麼意思？」 他們就說：「我只是說說」 「別帶你的量尺就對了」 好吧 我找到了個醫師 我第一次與Diana的會診

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: I brought in my list of the way the whole-hearted live, and I sat down. And she said, "How are you?" And I said, "I'm great. I'm okay." She said, "What's going on?" And this is a therapist who sees therapists,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我帶上我的清單 就是全心全意訪問清單，並坐下 她說：「你好嗎？」 我說：「我很好阿」 她說：「你怎麼了？」 我的心理醫師也看心理醫師

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: because we have to go to those, because their B.S. meters are good. (Laughter) And so I said, "Here's the thing, I'm struggling." And she said, "What's the struggle?" And I said, "Well, I have a vulnerability issue.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我的心理醫師也看心理醫師 因為每個人都有心事 然後他們胡扯的功力都很厲害 (笑聲) 然後我說 「是這樣的，我在掙扎」 她說：「掙扎什麼？」 我說：「我跟脆弱槓上了」 「我知道脆弱是丟臉和害怕」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And I know that vulnerability is the core of shame and fear and our struggle for worthiness, but it appears that it's also the birthplace of joy, of creativity, of belonging, of love. And I think I have a problem, and I need some help."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 「我知道脆弱是丟臉和害怕」 「的核心」 「為了自我價值的掙扎」 「但似乎也孕育出了」 「喜悅、創造力」 「歸屬和愛」 「所以我想我遇到困難了」 「我需要幫忙」 「但是，不要跟我講」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And I said, "But here's the thing: no family stuff, no childhood shit." (Laughter) "I just need some strategies." (Laughter) (Applause) Thank you. So she goes like this. (Laughter) And then I said, "It's bad, right?"
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 「但是，不要跟我講」 「我家庭怎樣」 「我童年怎樣的廢話」 (笑聲) 「告訴我怎麼做就好」 (笑聲) (掌聲) 謝謝 然後她就 (笑聲) 我問：「很糟吧？」 她答：「不好也不壞啦」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: And she said, "It's neither good nor bad." (Laughter) "It just is what it is." And I said, "Oh my God, this is going to suck." (Laughter) And it did, and it didn't. And it took about a year. And you know how there are people
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 她答：「不好也不壞啦」 (笑聲) 「不就這樣」 我說：「天哪，我完了」 (笑聲) 是也不是啦 我看了一年的醫生 你們知道有些人

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: that, when they realize that vulnerability and tenderness are important, that they surrender and walk into it. A: that's not me, and B: I don't even hang out with people like that. (Laughter) For me, it was a yearlong street fight.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 你們知道有些人 在明白脆弱與溫柔很重要之後 會舉旗投降 A: 這不是我的個性 B: 我不跟這種人交朋友 (笑聲) 對我來說，很像長達幾年的街頭鬥毆

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: It was a slugfest. Vulnerability pushed, I pushed back. I lost the fight, but probably won my life back. And so then I went back into the research and spent the next couple of years really trying to understand what they, the whole-hearted,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 打鬥很激烈 脆弱揍我，我打回去 我輸了 但卻贏了我的生活 所以我繼續這個研究 花了幾年時間 試著搞清楚這些全心全意的人 他們所做出的選擇

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: what choices they were making, and what we are doing with vulnerability. Why do we struggle with it so much? Am I alone in struggling with vulnerability? No. So this is what I learned. We numb vulnerability --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 他們所做出的選擇 他們怎麼應付 脆弱感的 為什麼我們如此掙扎？ 只有我與脆弱掙扎嗎？ 不是 這是我所學到的 我們麻痺脆弱

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: when we're waiting for the call. It was funny, I sent something out on Twitter and on Facebook that says, "How would you define vulnerability? What makes you feel vulnerable?" And within an hour and a half, I had 150 responses.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 例如我們在等待重要電話時 蠻好笑的，我在推特和臉書上打了 「要如何定義脆弱？」 「為什麼會感到脆弱？」 大概一個半小時後，我收到150個回覆 因為我想知道

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: Because I wanted to know what's out there. Having to ask my husband for help because I'm sick, and we're newly married; initiating sex with my husband; initiating sex with my wife; being turned down; asking someone out;
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 因為我想知道 大家的情形 找老公幫忙 因為我病了，而且才新婚、 向老公求歡、 向老婆求歡、 被拒絕、邀人去約會、 等著醫生回電、

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: waiting for the doctor to call back; getting laid off; laying off people. This is the world we live in. We live in a vulnerable world. And one of the ways we deal with it is we numb vulnerability. And I think there's evidence --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 等著醫生回電、 被辭退、辭退員工 -- 這就是我們的生活 我們生活在脆弱的世界裡 而我們應付脆弱的其中一個方式 就是麻痺脆弱感 我認為這是有證據的 證據存在不只是唯一理由

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: and it's not the only reason this evidence exists, but I think it's a huge cause -- We are the most in-debt ... obese ... addicted and medicated adult cohort in U.S. history. The problem is -- and I learned this from the research --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 證據存在不只是唯一理由 我認為這是美國史上 越來越多負債、 肥胖 成癮、依賴藥物、 成群的很大原因 問題是 -- 這是我從研究中學到的 -- 你無法選擇去麻痺情感

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: that you cannot selectively numb emotion. You can't say, here's the bad stuff. Here's vulnerability, here's grief, here's shame, here's fear, here's disappointment. I don't want to feel these. I'm going to have a couple of beers and a banana nut muffin.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 你無法選擇去麻痺情感 不能說，我不喜歡這感覺 脆弱感、悲傷、羞恥 害怕、失望等等 我不想要有這種感受 去喝啤酒和香蕉堅果鬆餅解悶 (笑聲)

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: (Laughter) I don't want to feel these. And I know that's knowing laughter. I hack into your lives for a living. God. (Laughter) You can't numb those hard feelings without numbing the other affects, our emotions.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: (笑聲) 麻痺這些感覺 我知道這笑聲 我把你們的生活都研究透徹了 天哪被發現了 (笑聲) 要麻痺難過的感覺 就要麻痹憐憫、情感 你不能選擇性麻痺

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: You cannot selectively numb. So when we numb those, we numb joy, we numb gratitude, we numb happiness. And then, we are miserable, and we are looking for purpose and meaning, and then we feel vulnerable,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 你不能選擇性麻痺 當我們麻痺這些 我們麻痺喜悅 我們麻痺感激 我們麻痺快樂 之後感到痛苦 然後追求目標與意義 之後感到脆弱 然後去喝啤酒和香蕉堅果鬆餅解悶

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: so then we have a couple of beers and a banana nut muffin. And it becomes this dangerous cycle. One of the things that I think we need to think about is why and how we numb. And it doesn't just have to be addiction.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 然後去喝啤酒和香蕉堅果鬆餅解悶 如此變成惡性循環 我們必須想想 我們為什麼、如何麻痺 並不一定是因為習慣了

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: The other thing we do is we make everything that's uncertain certain. Religion has gone from a belief in faith and mystery to certainty. "I'm right, you're wrong. Shut up." That's it. Just certain. The more afraid we are, the more vulnerable we are,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我們會做的另一件事 是確定那些令人不確定的事 宗教已從信仰與神秘 變成確定的事 我對、你錯、閉嘴 就這樣 就是確定性 我們越怕，就越脆弱 然後更害怕

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: the more afraid we are. This is what politics looks like today. There's no discourse anymore. There's no conversation. There's just blame. You know how blame is described in the research? A way to discharge pain and discomfort.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 然後更害怕 有點像現今的政治 沒有談話 沒有交談 只有責怪 你們知道研究上怎麼描述"責怪"嗎？ 釋放痛苦與不安的方式之一

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: We perfect. If there's anyone who wants their life to look like this, it would be me, but it doesn't work. Because what we do is we take fat from our butts and put it in our cheeks. (Laughter) Which just, I hope in 100 years, people will look back and go, "Wow."
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我們追求完美 世上唯有我希望生活如此 但不這麼稱心 我們選擇將屁股的脂肪抽出來 並拿去豐唇 (笑聲) 我希望幾百年後的人們 會對此瞠目咋舌 (笑聲)

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: (Laughter) And we perfect, most dangerously, our children. Let me tell you what we think about children. They're hardwired for struggle when they get here. And when you hold those perfect little babies in your hand,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: (笑聲) 我們追求完美時，最危險的 就是要孩子完美 跟你們講怎麼對待孩子吧 他們生來就得掙扎以達目的 當你手上抱著完美的寶寶時 我們的工作不是說：「看看她，真完美」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: our job is not to say, "Look at her, she's perfect. My job is just to keep her perfect -- make sure she makes the tennis team by fifth grade and Yale by seventh." That's not our job. Our job is to look and say,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我們的工作不是說：「看看她，真完美」 「我的工作是讓她保持完美」 「確定她五年級時選上網球隊，國一跳級念耶魯」 這不是我們的工作 我們的工作是看著他們說 「你知道嗎？你不完美，你生來就得掙扎」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: "You know what? You're imperfect, and you're wired for struggle, but you are worthy of love and belonging." That's our job. Show me a generation of kids raised like that, and we'll end the problems, I think, that we see today.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 「你知道嗎？你不完美，你生來就得掙扎」 「但你值得愛與歸屬的」 這才是我們的工作 如果一整代孩子都這麼養 那今日的問題都可迎刃而解 我們假裝我們所做的

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: We pretend that what we do doesn't have an effect on people. We do that in our personal lives. We do that corporate -- whether it's a bailout, an oil spill ... a recall. We pretend like what we're doing
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 我們假裝我們所做的 對他人沒有影響 我們在他人的生活中都是這麼做的 還會合作完成-- 不管是財政援助或油輪漏油、 產品回收-- 我們假裝我們所做的 對他人沒有極大的影響

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: doesn't have a huge impact on other people. I would say to companies, this is not our first rodeo, people. We just need you to be authentic and real and say ... "We're sorry. We'll fix it." But there's another way, and I'll leave you with this.
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 對他人沒有極大的影響 大公司們，我們沒這麼好騙 我們只要你們老實的說聲 「對不起」 「我們會彌補」 但還有別的方法的，我留此作結 這是我發現的：

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: This is what I have found: To let ourselves be seen, deeply seen, vulnerably seen ... to love with our whole hearts, even though there's no guarantee -- and that's really hard, and I can tell you as a parent, that's excruciatingly difficult --
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 這是我發現的： 讓自己被看見 最深層的那面 脆弱的那面 全心全意去愛 即使不保證有回報 即使很困難 尤其身為一個家長，我告訴你們，當我們恐懼時

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: to practice gratitude and joy in those moments of terror, when we're wondering, "Can I love you this much? Can I believe in this this passionately? Can I be this fierce about this?" just to be able to stop and, instead of catastrophizing what might happen,
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 表達感激與喜樂 是很痛徹心扉的 當我們想：「我能否這麼愛你？」 「我能熱情地相信嗎？」 「我可以對此勇敢嗎？」的時候 能夠停下來，別把一切想的太糟糕 然後告訴自己:「我很感激」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: to say, "I'm just so grateful, because to feel this vulnerable means I'm alive." And the last, which I think is probably the most important, is to believe that we're enough. Because when we work from a place, I believe, that says, "I'm enough" ...
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 然後告訴自己:「我很感激」 「因為會感到脆弱代表我還活著」 最後，我想最重要的是 相信自己足夠了 因為如果我們都能 相信自己「我夠好了」

brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.en: then we stop screaming and start listening, we're kinder and gentler to the people around us, and we're kinder and gentler to ourselves. That's all I have. Thank you. (Applause)
brene_brown_the_power_of_vulnerability.zh: 那我們便會停止抱怨並開始傾聽 我們對身邊的人會更溫柔仁慈 對自己也會更溫柔仁慈 就這樣，謝謝各位 (掌聲)

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: How do you explain when things don't go as we assume? Or better, how do you explain when others are able to achieve things that seem to defy all of the assumptions? For example: Why is Apple so innovative?
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 你會怎麼解釋 當事情不如我們所想的一般時？ 或者更好的是，你如何解釋 當其他人能夠完成似乎 違反所有假設的事時？ 比如： 為什麼蘋果電腦這麼地有創意？

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Year after year, after year, they're more innovative than all their competition. And yet, they're just a computer company. They're just like everyone else. They have the same access to the same talent,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 年復年 他們比其他的競爭對手都要有創意 然而，他們只是一個電腦公司 就像其他電腦公司一樣 他們對有同樣才華的人有相同的管道接觸 同樣的廣告商、同樣的顧問、同樣的媒體

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: the same agencies, the same consultants, the same media. Then why is it that they seem to have something different? Why is it that Martin Luther King led the Civil Rights Movement? He wasn't the only man who suffered in pre-civil rights America,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 同樣的廣告商、同樣的顧問、同樣的媒體 那麼為什麼他們 似乎有點與眾不同？ 為什麼是馬汀路德金恩 領導黑人民權運動？ 他不是唯一一個 在美國受到平等公民權不公平待遇的人

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and he certainly wasn't the only great orator of the day. Why him? And why is it that the Wright brothers were able to figure out controlled, powered man flight when there were certainly other teams who were better qualified, better funded --
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而且他絕不是當時最偉大的演說家 為什麼是他？ 而為什麼是華特兄弟 能夠想到控制力量，操控飛機 當正有其他團隊 更有資格、更有資本 然而他們並沒有完成飛機

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and they didn't achieve powered man flight, and the Wright brothers beat them to it. There's something else at play here. About three and a half years ago, I made a discovery. And this discovery profoundly changed my view on how I thought the world worked,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 然而他們並沒有完成飛機 反而是華特兄弟打敗他們完成了這件事。 這似乎有其他的因素在其中 大約三年半前 我有一個發現 而這個發現深深地改變 我對世界運作方式的看法

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and it even profoundly changed the way in which I operate in it. As it turns out, there's a pattern. As it turns out, all the great inspiring leaders and organizations in the world, whether it's Apple or Martin Luther King or the Wright brothers,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而它甚至深深改變 我生活的方式 因為它變成—有個模式 而它引證了，所有偉大且具有啟發性的領袖 以及世界上的機構 不管是蘋果電腦，或馬汀路德金恩或華特兄弟

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: they all think, act and communicate the exact same way. And it's the complete opposite to everyone else. All I did was codify it, and it's probably the world's simplest idea. I call it the golden circle.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們都以同樣的方式 思考、行動與溝通 而這也完全與其他人 相反 我做的事就是解讀它 而它或許是世界上 最簡單的想法 我稱它做黃金圈

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Why? How? What? This little idea explains why some organizations and some leaders are able to inspire where others aren't. Let me define the terms really quickly. Every single person, every single organization on the planet
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 為什麼？怎麼做？做什麼？ 這個小小的想法解釋了 為什麼有些機構和有些領袖 能夠激勵群眾而別人做不到 讓我很快地定義這些詞 在這個星球上的每個人，每個機構都

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: knows what they do, 100 percent. Some know how they do it, whether you call it your differentiated value proposition or your proprietary process or your USP. But very, very few people or organizations know why they do what they do.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 知道他們在做什麼　－ 百分之百肯定 有些知道如何做事 這裡可以叫做是你不同的價值主張 或者你的屬性過程或你的USP (賣點) 但很少很少人或機構 知道為什麼做他們正在做的事

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And by "why" I don't mean "to make a profit." That's a result. It's always a result. By "why," I mean: What's your purpose? What's your cause? What's your belief? Why does your organization exist? Why do you get out of bed in the morning?
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 知道為什麼做他們正在做的事 而〞為什麼〞不是指〞創造利潤“ 那是結果。它永遠是結果。 〞為什麼〞指的是：你的目的是什麼？ 你的出發點是什麼？你的信念是什麼？ 為什麼你的機構存在？ 為什麼你早上要起床？ 而且為什麼其他人要在乎？

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And why should anyone care? As a result, the way we think, we act, the way we communicate is from the outside in, it's obvious. We go from the clearest thing to the fuzziest thing. But the inspired leaders and the inspired organizations --
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而且為什麼其他人要在乎？ 好的，結果是我們所想、所為 以及我們所溝通的方式都是由外而內的 很明顯。我們從最清楚到最模糊不明 但激勵人的領導者 以及激勵人們的機構

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: regardless of their size, regardless of their industry -- all think, act and communicate from the inside out. Let me give you an example. I use Apple because they're easy to understand and everybody gets it.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 不管它們的規模，不管它們的產業 都以由內而外的方式 去思考、行動與溝通 讓我給你們舉個例子 我使用蘋果電腦是因為它很容易明白，而所有人都明白它 如果蘋果電腦就像其他的電腦

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: If Apple were like everyone else, a marketing message from them might sound like this: "We make great computers. They're beautifully designed, simple to use and user friendly. Want to buy one?" "Meh." That's how most of us communicate.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 如果蘋果電腦就像其他的電腦 給的行銷訊息很可能聽起來像 我們製造最棒的電腦 他們都有美好的設計、容易上手 也迎合使用者的要求 想買一台嗎？...呃，不要。 這就是多數人溝通的方式

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: That's how most marketing and sales are done, that's how we communicate interpersonally. We say what we do, we say how we're different or better and we expect some sort of a behavior, a purchase, a vote, something like that.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這就是多數行銷的方式。 這是多數業務銷售的方式 而這也是我們多數人與人相處時的溝通方式 我們說我們做了什麼，我們說我們如何不同或我們如何比較好 然後我們期待某種行為 可能是購買行為、投票行為或這類的事 這裡是我們新的法律公司

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Here's our new law firm: We have the best lawyers with the biggest clients, we always perform for our clients. Here's our new car: It gets great gas mileage, it has leather seats. Buy our car. But it's uninspiring.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這裡是我們新的法律公司 我們有最好的律師和最大的客戶 我們總是為和我們做生意的客戶著想 這是我們的新車 它有很好的油耗表現。它有皮革製的座椅。買我們的車吧。 但這都不激勵人心

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Here's how Apple actually communicates. "Everything we do, we believe in challenging the status quo. We believe in thinking differently. The way we challenge the status quo is by making our products beautifully designed,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這裡有蘋果電腦實際上溝通的方式： 我們所做的每件事 我們相信都在挑戰現狀 我們相信用不同的角度思考 我們挑戰現狀的方式 是使我們的產品有美好的設計

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: simple to use and user friendly. We just happen to make great computers. Want to buy one?" Totally different, right? You're ready to buy a computer from me. I just reversed the order of the information.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 容易使用而且迎合使用者。 我們只是恰巧做了很棒的電腦。 想要買一台嗎？ 完全不一樣，對吧？你準備要從我這裡買一台電腦了。 我只要改變資訊的順序 這個例子告訴我們人們不會買你做了什麼

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: What it proves to us is that people don't buy what you do; people buy why you do it. This explains why every single person in this room is perfectly comfortable buying a computer from Apple. But we're also perfectly comfortable
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這個例子告訴我們人們不會買你做了什麼 人們要買你為什麼做它 人們不會買你做什麼；他們要買你的為什麼。 這解釋了為什麼 在場的每個人 都對買蘋果電腦感到很好 但我們也同樣感覺很好

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: buying an MP3 player from Apple, or a phone from Apple, or a DVR from Apple. As I said before, Apple's just a computer company. Nothing distinguishes them structurally from any of their competitors. Their competitors are equally qualified to make all of these products.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 當購買蘋果電腦 的MP3播放器，或蘋果電腦的手機 或蘋果電腦的DVR 但如同我前述所說，蘋果電腦只是一家電腦公司 結構上沒有什麼使它們與 其他的競爭者不同 他們的競爭者都有同樣能力做這些產品 事實上，他們試過

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: In fact, they tried. A few years ago, Gateway came out with flat-screen TVs. They're eminently qualified to make flat-screen TVs. They've been making flat-screen monitors for years. Nobody bought one. Dell came out with MP3 players and PDAs,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 事實上，他們試過 幾年前，Gateway推出他們的平面電視 他們很明顯夠資格製造平面電視 他們已經做平面監視器好幾年了 沒有人買 戴爾電腦推出mp3播放器與PDA.

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and they make great quality products, and they can make perfectly well-designed products -- and nobody bought one. In fact, talking about it now, we can't even imagine buying an MP3 player from Dell. Why would you buy one from a computer company?
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們的產品都有很好的品質 而且他們也能完美地製造精美設計的產品 然而沒有人買。 事實上，現在我們說這些事，我們甚至無法想像 從戴爾電腦買一台mp3播放器 為什麼你會從一家電腦公司買一台mp3播放器？ 但我們每天都這麼做

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: But we do it every day. People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it. The goal is not to do business with everybody who needs what you have. The goal is to do business with people who believe what you believe.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 但我們每天都這麼做 人們不買你做什麼；他們買你的為什麼 目標不是 與每個需要你有什麼的人在做生意 目標是要與 那些相信你所相信的人做生意

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Here's the best part: None of what I'm telling you is my opinion. It's all grounded in the tenets of biology. Not psychology, biology. If you look at a cross-section of the human brain, from the top down, the human brain is actually broken
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這就是最美好的部份 我告訴你們的都不是我個人的意見 這在生物學裡證實 不是心理學，是生物學 如果你去看人腦裡的切面，從上往下看 你看到的人腦實際上是 分成三個主要部份的

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: into three major components that correlate perfectly with the golden circle. Our newest brain, our Homo sapien brain, our neocortex, corresponds with the "what" level. The neocortex is responsible for all of our rational and analytical thought and language.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 分成三個主要部份的 這和黃金圈完美地相關 我們最新的腦，或說我們人類的腦 我們的大腦皮層 對應的是〞什麼〞的層次 大腦皮層負責我們所有的 理性、分析的想法 以及語言

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: The middle two sections make up our limbic brains, and our limbic brains are responsible for all of our feelings, like trust and loyalty. It's also responsible for all human behavior, all decision-making,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 中間兩個部份組成我們腦的邊緣 而我們邊緣的腦是負責所有的感情 如信任與忠誠 它也同時負責所有人類的行為 所有的決策

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and it has no capacity for language. In other words, when we communicate from the outside in, yes, people can understand vast amounts of complicated information like features and benefits and facts and figures.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而它沒有語言的空間 換句話說，當我們從外而內地溝通時 是的，人們可以了解很多複雜的資訊量 像是特色、好處、事實與數字

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: It just doesn't drive behavior. When we can communicate from the inside out, we're talking directly to the part of the brain that controls behavior, and then we allow people to rationalize it with the tangible things we say and do.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 但這不能刺激行為 當我們能由內而外溝通時 我們就正對著我們的腦說話 那控制行為的腦 然後我們允許人們去分析它 以我們可以說與做的可見事物

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: This is where gut decisions come from. Sometimes you can give somebody all the facts and figures, and they say, "I know what all the facts and details say, but it just doesn't feel right." Why would we use that verb, it doesn't "feel" right?
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這就是我們用膽識做決定的來源 你們知道，有時候你們可以給人們 所有的事實與數字 然後他們說，〞我知道事實與細節是什麼， 但就是感覺不對勁〞 為什麼我們會用這個動詞，〝感覺〞不對勁？

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Because the part of the brain that controls decision-making doesn't control language. The best we can muster up is, "I don't know. It just doesn't feel right." Or sometimes you say you're leading with your heart or soul.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 為什麼我們會用這個動詞，〝感覺〞不對勁？ 因為管控決策的腦 並不管控語言 而我們最多能整理出的是〝我不知道，就是覺得不對〞 或者有時你說你隨心所欲 或者你跟隨你靈魂的聲音

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: I hate to break it to you, those aren't other body parts controlling your behavior. It's all happening here in your limbic brain, the part of the brain that controls decision-making and not language. But if you don't know why you do what you do,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 嗯，我很不想對你說，那些都不是身體的其他部份 在控制你的行為 所有的發生都在你邊緣的腦裡 是控制決策而不是語言的腦 但如果你不知道為什麼做你正在做的事

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and people respond to why you do what you do, then how will you ever get people to vote for you, or buy something from you, or, more importantly, be loyal and want to be a part of what it is that you do.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而人們對為什麼你正在做的事有反應 然後甚至是你如何贏得人們 的選票或買你的東西 或更重要的，忠實 而且想成為你正在做的事的一份子

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: The goal is not just to sell to people who need what you have; the goal is to sell to people who believe what you believe. The goal is not just to hire people who need a job; it's to hire people who believe what you believe.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 再一次，目標不是僅僅將需要你所有賣給需要的人而已 目標是要賣東西給那些相信你所相信的事的人 目標不是只在雇 那些需要工作的人 而是要雇用那些相信你所相信的事的人

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: I always say that, you know, if you hire people just because they can do a job, they'll work for your money, but if they believe what you believe, they'll work for you with blood and sweat and tears. Nowhere else is there a better example than with the Wright brothers.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 我會一直這麼說，你知道。 如果你雇用人僅是因為他們能做事，他們將會為了你的錢而做事 但如果你雇用那些相信你所相信的人們 他們將與你工作：用血汗與淚水。 而沒有比這個萊特兄弟 更好的例子了

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Most people don't know about Samuel Pierpont Langley. And back in the early 20th century, the pursuit of powered man flight was like the dot com of the day. Everybody was trying it. And Samuel Pierpont Langley had, what we assume,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 多數人不認識Samuel Pierpont Langley. 在20世紀初 追尋人類飛翔就像今天的網路公司一樣 每個人都在嘗試 而Samuel Pierpont Langley曾經擁有，我們假設的

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: to be the recipe for success. Even now, you ask people, "Why did your product or why did your company fail?" and people always give you the same permutation of the same three things: under-capitalized, the wrong people, bad market conditions.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 成功的關鍵 我的意思是，即使現在，你去問人們 〝為什麼你的產品為或什麼你的公司失敗？〞 而人們總會給你 相類似的三個原因 資金不足，不適合的人，市場情況不良
